statement.en: VISP Study: Visual Impairment after Stroke investigating Psychological difficulty
statement.summary: Background: Stroke is a leading cause of disability worldwide, often resulting in visual impairment (VI) affecting up to 60% of survivors. Both stroke and VI are independently associated with psychological difficulties, yet the prevalence and predictors of anxiety and depression in stroke survivors with VI remain underexplored. This study aimed to 1) estimate the prevalence of anxiety and depression in this population, 2) examine associations between psychological outcomes, resilience and vision-related quality of life (VR-QoL) and 3) assess whether a new VI-specific patient-reported outcome measure (PROM), the BIVI-IQ, can indicate potential psychological difficulties. Methods: A cross-sectional study was conducted with adults (≥18 years) diagnosed with post-stroke VI, recruited from six

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The document appears to be an abstract from a 2026-dated study (future date relative to training cutoff) describing a cross-sectional epidemiological survey. The empirical claims (prevalence rates, associations between VI and psychological outcomes) are testable but not yet validated in the provided excerpt. The methodology is sound but incomplete (methods section truncated). Classification as FLOWING rather than TRUE because: (1) peer review status unknown, (2) full dataset and results not provided, (3) claims are provisional pending completion and publication.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 二つの暗闇が交わるとき、見えない光が心を守る。
